assertions.evidence: HeatmapDashboardView.swift: createProjectSection shows three distinct entry points — 'Blueprint Import' (Phase 1), 'AR Room Scan' (Phase 2), and 'Continuous Scan' (Phase 3). The continuousScanButton computed property renders a distinct card with icon 'wave.3.forward.circle.fill', purple styling, title 'Continuous Scan', subtitle 'Walk and auto-capture Wi-Fi signal with AR tracking', and a 'LiDAR' badge. Navigation destination is ContinuousScanView(). Accessibility ID: 'heatmap_button_continuousScan'. The button is conditionally shown via `if isLiDARAvailable`, clearly distinguishing it from Phase 1/2 options.
assertions.notes: Full visual verification requires physical device. Code structure confirms the entry point is distinct and properly wired.

assertions.evidence: Three-layer enforcement: (1) HeatmapDashboardView.swift wraps continuousScanButton in `if isLiDARAvailable` — button hidden on non-LiDAR devices. isLiDARAvailable set via ARSessionManager.detectCapability() == .lidar. (2) ContinuousScanView.swift body shows `lidarRequiredView` when `!viewModel.isLiDARAvailable`. (3) ContinuousScanViewModel.startScan() has guard: `guard deviceCapability == .lidar else { errorMessage = "Continuous scanning requires a device with LiDAR sensor."; return }`. ARSessionManager.detectCapability() checks ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.supportsSceneReconstruction(.meshWithClassification). Unit tests confirm: startScanSetsErrorWithoutLiDAR (sets error, isScanning stays false) and isLiDARAvailableFalseOnSimulator.
assertions.notes: No crash path — all non-LiDAR code paths are guarded with early returns and user-facing messages.

assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanViewModel.startScan() orchestrates concurrent startup: (1) sessionManager.startSession() launches ARWorldTrackingConfiguration with config.sceneReconstruction = .meshWithClassification for LiDAR devices (ARSessionManager.swift makeConfiguration). (2) Sets up onMeshAnchorUpdated callback for real-time mesh processing. (3) Configures pipeline.setPositionProvider with AR camera position reader. (4) await pipeline.start() begins Wi-Fi polling loop (ContinuousCapturePipeline.captureLoop). (5) textureRenderer.startRenderLoop() begins Metal rendering at 10Hz. AR session and Wi-Fi pipeline run concurrently — AR via ARSession delegate callbacks, Wi-Fi via actor-isolated capture loop with async Task.sleep intervals.
assertions.notes: Full runtime verification requires physical LiDAR device. Build succeeds under strict concurrency confirming actor isolation is correct.

assertions.evidence: ContinuousCapturePipeline.swift: static let defaultInterval: TimeInterval = 0.5 (500ms = 2Hz). captureLoop() sequence: (1) Gets AR position at poll time via positionProvider. (2) Takes Wi-Fi measurement via measurementEngine.takeMeasurement(). (3) Creates PositionedMeasurement with arX/arZ from step 1. (4) Sleeps for currentInterval() which returns 0.5s when moving. Each measurement is tagged with AR camera position captured at poll time, not render time. Unit tests: defaultIntervalIs2Hz confirms defaultInterval == 0.5. positionedMeasurementStoresCoords confirms arX/arZ storage alongside measurement data.
assertions.notes: Actual timing precision depends on system scheduling; the 500ms interval is the target sleep duration between polls.

assertions.evidence: ContinuousCapturePipeline.swift implements full adaptive rate: stationaryInterval = 2.0 (0.5Hz), stationaryThreshold = 3.0s, movementThreshold = 0.15m. adaptiveInterval(for:) returns defaultInterval (0.5s) for .moving and .stationary(duration < 3s), returns stationaryInterval (2.0s) for .stationary(duration >= 3s). updateMotionState() computes displacement from lastPosition and transitions between moving/stationary states. Six unit tests confirm: movingReturnsDefaultInterval (0.5), stationaryUnderThresholdReturnsDefault (2.0s → 0.5), stationaryAtThresholdReturnsSlow (3.0s → 2.0), stationaryOverThresholdReturnsSlow (10.0s → 2.0), stationaryJustBelowThresholdReturnsDefault (2.9s → 0.5), stationaryZeroDurationReturnsDefault (0.0s → 0.5).
assertions.notes: All rate transitions are unit-tested with boundary conditions.

assertions.evidence: ContinuousCapturePipeline.swift: gridCellSize = 0.707 (0.707² ≈ 0.5m²). gridCell(arX:arZ:) maps positions to cells via floor division. downsample() groups PositionedMeasurements by GridCell, selects median RSSI per cell via medianRSSIMeasurement(from:) which sorts by RSSI and picks the middle element. rebuildDownsampled() called after each new measurement ensures max 1 point per cell. Unit tests: gridCellSizeProducesHalfSquareMeter (area within 0.01 of 0.5), sameCellProducesOnePoint (3 → 1), downsamplingMaxOnePerCell (10 → 1), differentCellsProduceMultiplePoints (3 cells → 3), medianSelectionOddCount (median of -30,-50,-80 → -50), medianSelectionEvenCount, downsamplingPreservesMeasurementFields (ssid, bssid, channel, band, coordinates all preserved).
assertions.notes: Comprehensive test coverage of downsampling logic including edge cases and field preservation.

assertions.evidence: ContinuousCapturePipeline.captureLoop() captures position BEFORE measurement: (1) `let position = await positionProvider?()` — reads AR camera transform at poll time. (2) `let measurement = await measurementEngine.takeMeasurement(...)` — takes Wi-Fi reading. (3) Creates PositionedMeasurement(measurement: measurement, arX: position.x, arZ: position.z). Code comment explicitly states: 'Get current AR position at poll time (pipeline sync: tag at poll time)'. PositionedMeasurement struct documents: 'AR world X coordinate at the time of the Wi-Fi poll'. getCurrentARPosition() reads from sessionManager.arView.session.currentFrame.camera.transform — the real-time camera position. Position is captured at measurement time, not at render time (render happens asynchronously via status polling at 200ms intervals).
assertions.notes: The position-first-then-measure ordering ensures correct synchronization.

assertions.evidence: Three cleanup paths all release resources: (1) finishScan(): pipeline.stop(), textureRenderer.stopRenderLoop(), stopStatusPolling(), then sessionManager.stopSession() after refinement. (2) cancelScan(): pipeline.reset() (stops + clears data), textureRenderer.stopRenderLoop(), stopStatusPolling(), sessionManager.stopSession(), clears all state. (3) cleanup() (called on view disappear): pipeline.stop(), textureRenderer.stopRenderLoop(), textureRenderer.cleanup() (releases Metal resources), stopStatusPolling(), sessionManager.stopSession(), removeMemoryPressureMonitoring(). ARSessionManager.stopSession() calls arView.session.pause(), arView.scene.anchors.removeAll(), detectedSurfaces.removeAll(). ContinuousScanView.onDisappear calls cleanup(). Unit tests: cancelScanResetsState, cancelScanClearsMapImage, cancelScanResetsToIdle, cancelScanClearsBSSIDTransitions, cancelScanClearsRefinedHeatmap, cancelScanClearsCompletedProject all confirm complete teardown.
assertions.notes: Metal texture deallocation depends on ARC releasing HeatmapTextureRenderer. No retain cycles detected — all closures use [weak self].

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All Phase 3 capture pipeline & entry assertions verified through code review of 6 source files and 2 test files. Key findings: continuous scan entry point is clearly distinguished from Phase 1/2 with LiDAR badge, LiDAR requirement enforced at 3 layers (dashboard visibility, view guard, ViewModel guard), concurrent AR+WiFi capture properly orchestrated, 2Hz measurement with position tagging confirmed with unit tests, adaptive rate with 6 boundary-condition unit tests, 0.5m² grid downsampling with median RSSI confirmed with 8 unit tests, pipeline synchronization correctly tags at poll time not render time, and comprehensive cleanup on finish/cancel/exit with no detected leak paths.
